_meta.name: feature-graph
_meta.status: DRAFT — v0.5 vocabulary grown from the REAL production parts in drawing-samples/ (IR-ADR-007): the 1-1 bracket's recorded VOCABULARY_GAP list (path sketch profile, offset datum plane, up_to_surface end condition, edge fillet + edge reference model, construction geometry); v0.5.1 adds the 1-2 flange's gap list (revolve with in-sketch axis, circular_pattern with datum-intersection axis + runtime seed-feature reference). Do NOT freeze before the MAT v1 validation set arrives; v0.6.0 adds the ASSEMBLY sub-vocabulary (component + mate — Phase B, ADR-047), grown from the real assembly samples in drawing-samples/assembly/. v0.7.0 (the FORWARD Feature Graph session, IR-ADR-017) deliberately overrides the grow-from-real-parts mantra ONCE to cover the FULL existing part tool surface: sweep, linear_pattern, ellipse/spline profile primitives, graph-level material, edge_flange simple length mode. Known tool-surface limits recorded there (revolve/sweep/loft are boss-only; linear pattern D2 unwired). v0.7.1 (audit-A1) adds NO vocabulary — it PROMOTES eight closed vocabulary sets that existed only as prose (assembly_node_types, mate_types, mate_alignments, anchor_kinds_face/edge, pattern_directions, chamfer_types, bend_positions, edge_flange_positions) plus the two grammar sets (body_producers, feature_producers) into covered_subset as real arrays, so the schema<->ir_schema.py contract test can machine-diff them. Motivation: circular_pattern's seed list had silently rotted to five stale types since 0.7.0 — prose cannot be tested.
_meta.role: CAD-NEUTRAL intermediate representation (IR). The host AI (Planner) emits this; the deterministic solidworks-compiler lowers it to tool calls. The model NEVER emits raw tool sequences.
_meta.doubles_as: Capability registry — the set of supported feature node types + params IS the capability list. The Planner validates intent against this single artifact (no separate chatty discovery protocol).

graph.schema_version: string (required — IR schema version this graph targets)
graph.units: string (fixed: 'meters' — all lengths are SI meters, matching execution layer)
graph.nodes: array of feature nodes (ordered; order defines build sequence — tree order is law, recipe.md C1)
graph.material: object (optional, PART graphs only — {name: 'exact library material name, e.g. 1060 Alloy', library?: 'material database, default SolidWorks Materials'}. Material is DOCUMENT state, not a tree feature, so it lives at graph level, never as a node; the compiler applies it via ONE set_part_material call AFTER the last node. Rejected on assembly graphs.)
graph._params: object (optional, additive, IGNORED by the compiler — the lifted parameter map {param_name: {node, field, value_si}} per recipe.md C3)

node_common.id: string (required — stable node id, e.g. 'n1'; used as a reference target by later nodes)
node_common.type: string (required — feature type discriminator; must be one of the registered types below)
node_common.ref: reference object (optional — where this feature attaches; see reference_model). Omitted = canonical default datum.

reference_model._note: Still the make-or-break part of the IR (persistent/topological naming, P1.3). v0.5 adds GEOMETRIC ANCHORS (decided with the user 2026-07-04, HYBRID form): a 3D point recorded from the ORIGINAL part's analyze_model read, resolved DETERMINISTICALLY against the rebuilt geometry at compile time, plus an optional semantic 'hint' that the compiler NEVER reads — it collects design data for the future semantic reference model (P1.3). Anchors are exact for fresh-doc replay but NOT durable across edits — that honest limitation is the P1.3 forcing function.
reference_model.offset: number (optional, on a datum ref — SIGNED offset in meters along the datum normal; != 0 means the sketch sits on a parallel reference plane the compiler creates via add_reference_geometry, e.g. the recipe's plane {ref:'Front Plane', offset:0.003})

node_face.node: string — id of an earlier node
node_face.selector: string — semantic face selector resolved at compile time by the reference resolver: 'top' (v0.5: only 'top')

reference_model.position: string — semantic position on the referenced entity: 'center' (v0.5: only 'center')

face_anchor.near: [x, y, z] — a 3D point (meters) ON the target face's plane, from the ORIGINAL part's analyze_model(faces) representative point. Resolved by plane-containment match (|(near-point)·normal| <= tol) with nearest-representative-point tiebreak among coplanar faces.
face_anchor.hint: string (optional — semantic description for P1.3 data collection, e.g. 'top of the 3mm base plate'; NOT read by the compiler)

edge_anchor.near: [x, y, z] — a 3D point (meters) at the target edge's MIDPOINT, from the ORIGINAL part's analyze_model(edges) mid. Resolved by nearest-mid within a tolerance ball; >1 edge in the ball = REFERENCE_AMBIGUOUS, 0 = REFERENCE_UNRESOLVED.
edge_anchor.hint: string (optional — semantic description for P1.3 data collection, e.g. 'slot inner corner'; NOT read by the compiler)

covered_subset._note: The subset pycompiler compiles today (key renamed from v05_covered_subset at 0.7.0 — the version lives in _meta, not the key). Outside it the compiler rejects the graph at structural-validation time (no silent gaps). Superset of the v0-exp subset (box+hole fixture still compiles unchanged). CONTRACT (0.7.1, audit-A1): every ARRAY in this block is machine-diffed against the matching frozenset in solidworks-compiler/pycompiler/ir_schema.py by pycompiler/tests/test_ir_schema_contract.py — both directions, exact set equality. Adding a token on one side alone FAILS CI. The prose keys (_note, datum_offset, assumptions) are deliberately NOT contract; per-param types/ranges live in ir_schema.py code and are exercised by the pycompiler suite, not by string-parsing this file.
covered_subset.datum_offset: any signed number of meters (0/omitted = the datum plane itself)
covered_subset._grammar_note: body_producers = node types whose body a 'hole' may reference (ref.node_face.node). feature_producers = node types that CREATE a named feature, so they are valid seeds for linear_pattern/circular_pattern/mirror (the compiler substitutes the seed's runtime feature name). Patterns are producers themselves — that is what lets a grid compose as a pattern OF a pattern.
covered_subset.assumptions: box centred on the datum origin ('center' -> sketch (0,0)); 'top' bound to global +Y; single body. Path-profile sketches carry EXACT 2D coordinates (from analyze_model(sketch)) — the sketch plane's 2D frame is assumed identical between original and rebuild (same datum/offset => same frame). General 3D-point->sketch-2D resolution for arbitrary faces remains P1.5 option B.

feature_types._note: Profile and operation are SEPARATED on purpose (avoids combinatorial type explosion like 'extruded_circle' + 'extruded_rectangle' + 'revolved_circle'...). Key renamed from feature_types_v05 at 0.7.0 — the version lives in _meta.

box.description: Rectangular boss convenience node — a single IR node that the compiler lowers to many tool calls (create_sketch on the datum + a centred rectangle + a boss extrude). The clearest demonstration of one feature -> many ops.
box.ref: datum (the base plane the box is built on; default 'top')
box.width: number — meters (along the datum's local X)
box.depth: number — meters (along the datum's local Y)

sketch.description: A 2D profile on a plane. Entities are profile primitives, not tools. v0.5 adds PATH profiles: free line/arc chains with exact coordinates (the recipe's 7-line+2-arc bracket outline), plus construction scaffolding. Primitives sharing identical endpoint coordinates close the contour — do NOT add constraints (frozen-coordinate rebuild, see add_sketch_entity guidance).
sketch.ref: datum + optional offset (the sketch plane; offset != 0 => the compiler prefers an existing face on that plane, else creates a parallel reference plane) OR — since v0.5.5, for sketches on NON-axis-aligned support (a bent flange's face, 4-1's Sketch13/16) — { face: face_anchor }: the compiler resolves the anchor via analyze_model(faces) plane-containment (resolve_face_by_anchor) and sketches ON that face (create_sketch on_face). Record the ORIGINAL sketch's `frame` — the frame transform (IR-ADR-010) makes the 2D coordinates support-independent.

line._note: exact 2D sketch coords in meters (from analyze_model(sketch))

arc.dir: 1 (CCW) | -1 (CW) — sweep sense, REQUIRED (centre+start+end alone describe two arcs); mirrors analyze_model(sketch)'s 'dir' and add_sketch_entity(arc_center)'s 'direction'

ellipse.x1: number (X of a point on the MAJOR axis)
ellipse.x2: number (X of a point on the MINOR axis)
ellipse._note: COVERED since v0.7.0. Exactly the shape analyze_model(sketch) reports and add_sketch_entity(ellipse) consumes — round-trips verbatim.

spline.points: array of FLAT through-point coordinates [x1, y1, x2, y2, ...] — >= 2 points (4 numbers), even count
spline._note: COVERED since v0.7.0. Mirrors analyze_model(sketch)'s spline 'points' (flat x,y list). HONEST CAVEAT (KNOWN-LIMITATIONS): recreating a spline from through-points is VISUALLY equivalent, not bit-identical (SW stores control points + tangency, not interpolation points) — volume/area round-trips within tolerance, never byte-exact.

profile_primitives._common_flag: any primitive may carry construction: true — created as CONSTRUCTION/reference geometry (mirrors analyze_model's per-segment flag)

extrude.description: Solid extrusion of a referenced sketch node. v0.5 adds explicit end conditions.
extrude.sketch: string — id of a sketch node (must be the immediately preceding node — the extrude consumes the ACTIVE sketch)
extrude.end: string (optional) — 'blind' (default; requires depth) | 'through_all' | 'up_to_surface' (requires up_to) | 'mid_plane' (requires depth = the TOTAL width; extrudes symmetrically about the sketch plane). Back-compat: depth:'through_all'/'through' or through:true still mean through_all.
extrude.depth: number — meters (blind: the one-sided depth; mid_plane: the TOTAL symmetric width)
extrude.up_to: object (up_to_surface only) — { face: face_anchor } : the face the feature terminates on, as a geometric anchor (+ optional hint)

revolve.description: Solid of revolution of the referenced sketch node (COVERED since v0.5.1 — grown for the 1-2 flange). The profile sketch should contain the axis segment (typically the construction centerline of the half-section, the classic revolve idiom); the compiler passes the axis segment's 2D coordinates and the tool selects it in the active sketch and treats it as the centerline.
revolve.sketch: string — id of a sketch node (must be the immediately preceding node — the revolve consumes the ACTIVE sketch)
revolve.axis: { x1, y1, x2, y2 } — the axis SEGMENT's exact 2D sketch coordinates (meters), from analyze_model(sketch); normally the sketch's construction centerline
revolve.angle: number — RADIANS (default 6.283185 = 360°, matching the recipe's SI dimension; the compiler converts to the tool boundary's degrees, C4)

sweep.description: Swept solid (boss) along a path sketch (COVERED since v0.7.0 — forward-vocabulary session, IR-ADR-017). Lowers to extrude_feature(sweep). GRAMMAR: the PROFILE sketch node must IMMEDIATELY precede the sweep (the tool consumes the ACTIVE sketch as the profile, like extrude); the PATH is an EARLIER sketch node referenced by id — the compiler substitutes its RUNTIME sketch name (node_feature mechanism, localization/numbering-proof, same as loft's profiles). So the node order is: path sketch, ..., profile sketch, sweep. Boss-only (the execution surface has no sweep cut).
sweep.sketch: string — id of the PROFILE sketch node (must be the immediately preceding node)
sweep.path: string — id of an EARLIER sketch node holding the sweep path (an open line/arc chain; must not be the profile sketch)

rib.description: Rib feature from an OPEN profile sketch (COVERED since v0.5.2 — grown for the 2-1 angle bracket; required the NEW create_rib execution tool). The profile is typically a single line whose extensions hit existing walls (e.g. the diagonal between an L-bracket's legs); SolidWorks extends and thickens it.
rib.sketch: string — id of a sketch node (must be the immediately preceding node — the rib consumes the ACTIVE sketch)
rib.two_sided: boolean (optional, default true — thicken symmetrically about the sketch)
rib.reverse_thickness_dir: boolean (optional, default false — SINGLE-SIDED ribs only (two_sided=false): thicken the opposite side of the sketch normal; passthrough added v0.7.0)
rib.reverse_material_dir: boolean (optional, default false — which side the rib fills toward; a wrong value is auto-recovered by the tool's flipped retry)
rib.is_norm_to_sketch: boolean (optional, default false — false = extrusion parallel to the sketch, the classic line rib)

linear_pattern.description: Linear feature pattern along a canonical axis (COVERED since v0.7.0 — forward-vocabulary session, IR-ADR-017). Lowers to create_pattern(linear); the seed is an earlier feature-producing node whose RUNTIME feature name the compiler substitutes (node_feature, same as circular_pattern). SINGLE direction by design: the execution tool's documented count2/spacing2 second direction was never wired in C# (no D2 entity is selected — recorded tool-surface gap); a GRID composes as a pattern OF a pattern (linear_pattern seeding on an earlier linear_pattern node).
linear_pattern.feature: string — id of an earlier feature-producing node: the pattern seed
linear_pattern.direction: string — 'x' | 'y' | 'z' (canonical model axis; the tool selects the default plane whose normal is that axis)
linear_pattern.spacing: number — meters (> 0): distance between adjacent instances
linear_pattern.count: integer >= 2 — total instances incl. the seed
linear_pattern.flip: boolean (optional, default false — pattern toward the NEGATIVE axis; exposed v0.7.0 via FeatureLinearPattern3's FlipDir1)

circular_pattern.description: Circular feature pattern (COVERED since v0.5.1). References an EARLIER feature-producing node as the seed — the compiler substitutes that node's RUNTIME-created feature name (localization-proof, same principle as the offset-plane name) — and creates the pattern axis from two datum planes' intersection.
circular_pattern.feature: string — id of an earlier feature-producing node (see covered_subset.feature_producers for the registered list): the pattern seed
circular_pattern.axis: { datums: [d1, d2] } — two DISTINCT canonical datums ('front'|'top'|'right') whose intersection is the pattern axis (front∩right = the Y axis, etc.); the compiler creates the reference axis and threads its runtime name
circular_pattern.count: integer >= 2 — total instances incl. the seed
circular_pattern.angle_deg: number — DEGREES; with equal_spacing=true the TOTAL spread (360 = full ring), else the spacing BETWEEN instances (reproduce the recipe's CirPattern verbatim: count=instances, angle_deg=spacing_deg, equal_spacing as reported — see create_pattern's round-trip guidance)

hole.description: Parametric hole. Encapsulates sketch+cut; the compiler expands it (resolve top face -> create_sketch(on_face, face_index) -> circle at centre -> through cut).
hole.ref: node_face + position (e.g. top face of n1, center)
hole.depth: number — meters, or 'through_all' (v0.5 supports 'through_all' only; 'through' accepted as an alias)

fillet.description: 3D edge fillet (post-solid, NOT a sketch fillet). Lowers to add_edge_feature(fillet, edge_indices) after the resolver matches each edge anchor against live analyze_model(edges) midpoints. THE reference-model testbed — every lesson here feeds P1.3.
fillet.edges: array of edge_anchor objects (>= 1): [{ near: [x,y,z], hint?: string }] — one per filleted edge, midpoints from the ORIGINAL part's analyze_model(edges)

chamfer.description: 3D edge chamfer (post-solid; COVERED since v0.5.3 — grown for level-2-2). Same HYBRID edge-anchor reference model as fillet (IR-ADR-008): each anchor's `near` is the target edge's midpoint from the PRE-chamfer geometry (the chamfer consumes those edges), matched against live analyze_model(edges) on the rebuild. Lowers to add_edge_feature(chamfer, edge_indices).
chamfer.distance: number — meters (> 0): the first-face setback (D1), always required
chamfer.angle: number — RADIANS (distance_angle only; default 0.785398 = 45°; the compiler converts to the tool boundary's degrees, C4)
chamfer.distance2: number — meters (> 0; distance_distance only, required there): the second-face setback (D2)
chamfer.flip: boolean (optional, default false; distance_distance is directional — flip swaps which face gets D1 vs D2, auto-recoverable like rib's reverse)
chamfer.edges: array of edge_anchor objects (>= 1): [{ near: [x,y,z], hint?: string }] — one per chamfered edge, midpoints from the PRE-chamfer state's analyze_model(edges)

loft.description: Multi-profile boss loft / blend (COVERED since v0.5.4 — grown for level-2-2's Loft2 frustum). Lowers to extrude_feature(loft, profiles=[...]). UNLIKE extrude/revolve/rib, the loft selects its profile sketches BY NAME (not the active sketch), so the profile sketch nodes need NOT immediately precede the loft — they are ordinary earlier sketch nodes (each on its own plane/offset) that the loft references by id. The compiler threads each referenced sketch's RUNTIME name (recorded from create_sketch's activeSketch — localization/numbering-proof, same principle as circular_pattern's seed). v0.5.4 = a simple boss blend (no guide curves / start-end tangency); those are future growth.
loft.profiles: array of >= 2 sketch node ids, IN LOFT ORDER — the profiles the loft blends through (e.g. ["n10","n11"] for a two-circle frustum). Each must reference an EARLIER sketch node.

sheet_metal.description: Sheet-metal BASE FLANGE (COVERED since v0.5.5 — grown for 4-1). Converts the referenced sketch profile into the sheet blank and establishes the sheet definition (thickness / default bend radius / K-factor). Lowers to sheet_metal_feature(base_flange). The tree's SheetMetal definition feature and Flat-Pattern are byproducts — they are NOT separate IR nodes.
sheet_metal.sketch: string — id of a sketch node (must be the immediately preceding node — the base flange consumes the ACTIVE sketch)
sheet_metal.bend_radius: number (optional — default bend radius in meters; omitted = thickness)
sheet_metal.reverse_thickness: boolean (optional, default false — thicken to the OPPOSITE side of the sketch plane. A wrong side puts every downstream bend-sketch plane off the sheet. PREFER reproducing the original's own flag (analyze_model(features) reports it on the SMBaseFlange) over deriving from face positions — see symmetric_thickness for why)
sheet_metal.symmetric_thickness: boolean (optional, default false — thicken BOTH ways off the sketch plane, ±t/2 mid-plane style. Reproduce the ORIGINAL's own flag (analyze_model(features) reports it on the SMBaseFlange): the thickness-direction flags also define the intrinsic sheet orientation that downstream bend directions fold against — an identical blank B-rep built via reverse flags folded 4-1's Bend1 the MIRRORED way)

sketched_bend.description: Sketched bend (SM3dBend; COVERED since v0.5.5 — grown for 4-1's five bends). Bends the sheet about the bend LINE(S) in the referenced sketch — a sketch with N bend lines yields ONE feature with N bends (4-1's Sketch6). Lowers to sheet_metal_feature(sketched_bend). The FIXED side is a 3D pick point: SolidWorks derives which half stays put from the POINT, so this anchor carries side semantics, not just face identity.
sketched_bend.sketch: string — id of a sketch node holding the bend line(s) (must be the immediately preceding node — the bend consumes the ACTIVE sketch)
sketched_bend.angle: number — RADIANS (default 1.570796 = 90°, the recipe's SI dimension; the compiler converts to the tool boundary's degrees, C4)
sketched_bend.radius: number (optional — bend radius in meters; OMITTED = the sheet's default radius, matching a recipe sketched_bend block with no radius)
sketched_bend.flip: boolean (optional, default false — reverse the bend direction; replay the recipe's `flip`)
sketched_bend.fixed: { near: [x,y,z], hint?: string } — a 3D point (meters) ON the sheet face, on the SIDE that must stay put, in the PRE-bend geometry (the state the rebuild is in when the bend executes). Passed straight through as the tool's fixed_x/y/z coordinate pick (no index resolution — the point IS the side selector).

mirror.description: Feature mirror about a canonical datum plane (MirrorPattern; COVERED since v0.5.5 — grown for 4-1's Mirror1, which mirrors its two edge flanges about Right Plane). Lowers to create_pattern(mirror) with the referenced nodes' RUNTIME feature names (node_feature mechanism, localization-proof). NOTE (live-verified): SolidWorks refuses to mirror a bare SM3dBend sketched bend — mirror flanges/cuts/bosses; 4-1 mirrors edge flanges.
mirror.plane: { datum: 'front' | 'top' | 'right' } — the mirror plane (canonical; the compiler passes the canonical English name, resolved language-independently)
mirror.features: array of >= 1 earlier feature-producing node ids — the features to mirror, in tree order

edge_flange.description: Edge flange (EdgeFlange; COVERED since v0.5.6 — 4-1's last vocabulary gap). TWO MODES since v0.7.0: (A) CUSTOM PROFILE (the reverse-replay mode) — frame + profile given. SELF-CONTAINED node: no preceding sketch node — the flange API only accepts a profile sketch IT generated (InsertSketchForEdgeFlange), so the compiler generates it, CLEARS the default rectangle, redraws `profile`, then creates the flange (sheet_metal_feature edge_flange_sketch → add_sketch_entity* → edge_flange_finish). The generated sketch's frame is UNPREDICTABLE, hence `frame` is REQUIRED there (IR-ADR-010 transform). (B) SIMPLE LENGTH (the forward mode, v0.7.0/IR-ADR-017) — `length` given INSTEAD of frame+profile: a full-edge-width flange of that length, lowered to ONE sheet_metal_feature(edge_flange) call with the anchor-resolved edge_index. Length mode accepts only edge + angle + length (position stays the tool default material_inside, radius = the sheet default, flip unsupported — use mode A when you need those).
edge_flange.edge: { near: [x,y,z], hint?: string } — a 3D point (meters) ON the attach edge, in the geometry state when this node executes (the reader's edge_flange.edges[].mid — ground-truth readback, IR-ADR-014)
edge_flange.angle: number — RADIANS (default 1.570796 = 90°; the compiler converts to the tool boundary's degrees, C4)
edge_flange.length: number — meters (> 0; MODE B only, mutually exclusive with frame/profile): the flange length along the bend direction
edge_flange.radius: number (optional, MODE A only — bend radius in meters; OMITTED = the sheet's default radius)
edge_flange.position: string (optional, MODE A only — 'material_inside' (tool default) | 'material_outside' | 'bend_outside' | 'centerline' | 'bend_sharp'; replay the recipe edge_flange block's `position` verbatim — 4-1's Edge-Flange1 is bend_outside)
edge_flange.flip: boolean (optional, MODE A only, default false — InsertSketchForEdgeFlange's FlipDir; at 90° the flange plane is flip-invariant and the frame transform absorbs the orientation, but replay it when read)
edge_flange.frame: { origin, xdir, ydir } — the ORIGINAL profile sketch's frame (REQUIRED in mode A, ABSENT in mode B)
edge_flange.profile: array of profile primitives — the ORIGINAL profile sketch's segments, original 2D coords (line/arc/circle + construction flags, same grammar as sketch.profile; REQUIRED in mode A, ABSENT in mode B)

validation_split.plan_time_planner: Structural + schema only: type is registered, required params present, node references (sketch/node ids) structurally resolvable, units correct, anchors well-formed ([x,y,z] numbers), arc dir in {1,-1}.
validation_split.compile_exec_time: Geometric validity: referenced face/edge actually exists after prior features, anchors resolve to exactly one entity (tolerance ball), hole fits within the face. Owned by compiler + reference resolver + execution — NOT the Planner.

example._intent: Create a 20mm hole at the center of the top face of a 100x100x20mm block.

example_v05_new_vocabulary._intent: Fragment showing every v0.5 addition at once: a path-profile sketch (line+arc+construction) on an OFFSET plane, a cut terminating up-to-surface, and an anchored edge fillet.

face.hint: top face of the 3mm base plate

assembly_types_v06._note: ASSEMBLY sub-vocabulary (v0.6.0 — Phase B, ADR-047). An assembly graph contains ONLY these types: components first (tree order), then mates (creation order). Components reference part FILES by path+hash (the V1 cache discipline extends) — part IRs are NEVER embedded. Out of scope first round (each hit = VOCABULARY_GAP, C5): in-context features, flexible subassemblies, component patterns, configurations beyond the referenced one.

component.description: One component OCCURRENCE (instance). Lowers to insert_component (AddComponent5) with the FULL transform; `fixed` is always sent explicitly (deterministic state).
component.source: { path: '<part file>', hash?: 'sha256:...' } — the part FILE this occurrence references; hash makes staleness loud (ADR-040)
component.fixed: boolean (optional, default false) — fixed occurrences take `transform` as their authoritative PLACEMENT; floating ones use it as the initial placement + verification ground truth (mates do the constraining)
component.transform: REQUIRED — 13 numbers: 3x3 rotation row-major + translation xyz (meters) + scale — exactly analyze_assembly(components)' reported layout

mate.description: One mate between two component occurrences. Lowers to add_mate (AddMate5) with INDEX-first entity selection: each side's COMPONENT-LOCAL anchor resolves to a face/edge index via analyze_assembly(faces|edges, component) (KNOWN-LIMITATIONS #6). Read the original's type/alignment/value from the ENUMS verbatim (C7) — display names are localized and unreliable; never derive them from resulting positions.
mate.alignment: 'aligned' | 'anti_aligned' | 'closest' (optional; record the reader's RESOLVED alignment verbatim)
mate.value: number (SI: meters for distance, RADIANS for angle — C4; the compiler converts to the tool boundary) — required for distance/angle
mate.sides: EXACTLY 2 × { component: <earlier component node id>, anchor: { kind: 'plane'|'cylinder'|'cone'|'sphere'|'line'|'circle' (the reader's swMateEntity2ReferenceType_e name, verbatim), near: [x,y,z] COMPONENT-LOCAL meters (map the original mate's assembly-space EntityParams through the inverse component transform — local anchors are transform-invariant), hint?: string } }
